subtasks.description: Query IAM role registry for all roles with elevated permissions (admin, power-user, or custom elevated policy definitions). Return role ID, name, attached policies, and permission grants.

Query CloudTrail or IAM activity logs for the last 180 days to extract which permissions (action:resource pairs) were actually invoked by each IAM role. Return role ID, exercised actions, resource ARNs, invocation timestamps.

Cross-reference granted permissions (from iam_granted_roles) against exercised permissions (from iam_exercised_perms). Identify permissions granted but never invoked in 180 days. For each elevated role, produce a summary of revocation candidates with granted-but-unused action:resource tuples, last invocation dates for used permissions, and risk assessment (unused admin vs. unused read-only).

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks with minimum cardinality: (1) Query IAM role definitions (mid-tier SQL to handle multi-policy joins and elevated permission filtering); (2) Query CloudTrail activity logs for 180-day window (mid-tier SQL for time-windowed log aggregation); (3) Cross-reconcile the two datasets with differential analysis, risk classification, and revocation recommendation generation (frontier-tier cross_recon to handle complex set operations, posture classification of unused elevated permissions, and downstream actionability assessment). This avoids unnecessary duplication and leverages cross_recon's capability to synthesize granted vs. exercised state into a coherent security posture decision.

output.sql: SELECT al.user_id, al.permission, al.resource, COUNT(*) as exercise_count, MAX(al.access_time) as last_access FROM access_log_facts al WHERE al.access_time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '180 days' AND al.permission IN ('admin', 'power-user') AND al.result = 'success' GROUP BY al.user_id, al.permission, al.resource ORDER BY al.user_id, exercise_count DESC

SELECT user_id, permission, resource, action, access_time, COUNT(*) as invocation_count FROM access_log_facts WHERE access_time >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '180 days' AND result = 'success' GROUP BY user_id, permission, resource, action, access_time ORDER BY user_id, access_time DESC

reconciled.note: MongoDB source not provided; using SQL as primary
MongoDB source not provided; using SQL as primary

reconciled.sql_value: LEFT JOIN: granted_roles LEFT JOIN exercised_perms ON (user_id, permission, resource)
reconciled.note: Identify all granted perms; match against exercised; unmatched = revocation candidates

reconciled.sql_value: granted_roles entries with NO corresponding exercised_perms match in 180d window
reconciled.risk_assessment: CRITICAL if permission='admin'; MEDIUM if permission='power-user'

reconciled.sql_value: matched (user_id, permission, resource) with last_access from iam_granted_roles + invocation_count, last access_time from iam_exercised_perms
admin perms in granted_roles with zero matches in exercised_perms
